message:
refactor(rpc): reuse ProcessGuard in RPC bash, widen ProcessGuard visibility

src/rpc.rs:
- Replace manual child_pid tracking + kill_process_tree + child.kill()
  with ProcessGuard::new(child, true) for consistent process lifecycle
  management in run_bash_rpc(). The guard ensures kill-on-drop cleanup
  even if the RPC handler panics or is cancelled.
- Use guard.kill() and guard.try_wait_child() instead of direct child
  method calls, unifying RPC bash with the existing tool bash code path

src/tools.rs:
- Widen ProcessGuard methods (new, try_wait_child, kill, wait) from
  private to pub(crate) so rpc.rs can reuse the same guard
- Simplify map_normalized_range_to_original() iterator: replace
  .peekable() + while-let-next with plain for-loop since peek was
  never used in the loop body

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
